outputs_spec: txd_o toggles per UART frame: start(0) + DATA_WIDTH LSB-first data + optional parity + STOP_BITS stop(1)
side_effects_spec: Head byte popped from TX FIFO
tx_empty status updated (1 if FIFO now empty)
result.ssot_question: [SSOT QUESTION] structured output_rules/state_updates undefined for transaction FM_TX

outputs_spec: Received DATA_WIDTH byte pushed into RX FIFO on success
STATUS flags updated: frame_err, parity_err, rx_overrun as applicable
side_effects_spec: Received byte pushed into RX FIFO if not full
bytes_rx incremented by 1 on success (wraps at 2^32)
result.ssot_question: [SSOT QUESTION] structured output_rules/state_updates undefined for transaction FM_RX

outputs_spec: txd_o toggles normally AND rxd_i sampling path receives txd_o instead of external rxd_i (loopback mux before synchronizer)
side_effects_spec: Byte circulates: TX FIFO → TX FSM → loopback mux → RX synchronizer → RX FSM → RX FIFO
result.ssot_question: [SSOT QUESTION] structured output_rules/state_updates undefined for transaction FM_LOOPBACK

invariants_skipped.name: TX FSM and RX FSM operate independently
invariants_skipped.expr: TX FSM and RX FSM operate independently except for loopback mux sharing.

invariants_skipped.name: Register read side effects are exactly t
invariants_skipped.expr: Register read side effects are exactly those listed in registers.register_list.

invariants_skipped.name: Sticky error flags (frame_err, parity_er
invariants_skipped.expr: Sticky error flags (frame_err, parity_err, rx_overrun, tx_underrun, break_detected) latch on first event and hold until W1C via INT_CLEAR.

invariants_skipped.name: Debug counters are free-running and do n
invariants_skipped.expr: Debug counters are free-running and do not roll over to affect core function.

invariants_skipped.name: FIFO depth is parameter FIFO_DEPTH; writ
invariants_skipped.expr: FIFO depth is parameter FIFO_DEPTH; writes to full FIFO are ignored; reads from empty FIFO return 0.

authority_contract.rule: LLM loops are allowed against general machine-checkable criteria (diffs, diagnostics, coverage gaps, traceability gaps, stale evidence, assertions, and measurements). Intended behavior, oracle semantics, waivers, interface changes, performance tradeoffs, and signoff require human approval.
general_evaluation_criteria: traceability: every generated artifact traces to SSOT refs
functional_equivalence: RTL observables match FunctionalModel results
module_equivalence: behavior-owning modules have module-boundary evidence
coverage_closure: SSOT coverage goals are hit by passing RTL-observed evidence
interface_protocol: io_list/cycle_model handshakes, reset, ordering, and errors hold
lint_compile: DUT-only compile/lint has zero unwaived diagnostics
simulation_evidence: results, scoreboard rows, and coverage are fresh and structured
performance_cycle: cycle/performance targets are measured or escalated
maintainability: no fixed IP workaround; modules/TB remain SSOT-owned and reviewable
human_decision: intent, waivers, oracle changes, tradeoffs, and final signoff are human-owned
loopable_evidence_points: FL expected vs RTL actual diff
module FL expected vs RTL boundary actual diff
coverage goal vs coverage result gap
CL target vs measured performance gap
loopable_oracles: FL expected vs RTL actual diff
module FL expected vs RTL boundary actual diff
coverage goal vs coverage result gap
CL target vs measured performance gap
